title: The Bishops of Rome in those Times.

source.title: NPNF2-01. Eusebius Pamphilius: Church History, Life of Constantine, Oration in Praise of Constantine

verses.text: Cornelius, having held the episcopate in the city of Rome about three years, was succeeded by Lucius.
He died in less than eight months, and transmitted his office to Stephen.
Dionysius wrote to him the first of his letters on baptism, as no small controversy had arisen as to whether those who had turned from any heresy should be purified by baptism.
For the ancient custom prevailed in regard to such, that they should receive only the laying on of hands with prayers.
